Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Main article: 2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup

The 2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup was an international football tournament co-hosted by Fiji and Vanuatu from 15 to 30 June 2024. The seven national teams involved in the tournament were required to submit squads of maximum 23 players – of which three had to be goalkeepers (Regulation 21.1). Only players in these squads were eligible to take part in the tournament.[1]

The age listed for each player is on 15 June 2024, the first day of the tournament. The number of caps listed for each player does not include any matches played after the start of the 2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup.

Group A

[edit]

New Zealand

[edit]

Head coach: Darren Bazeley

New Zealand announced their final squad, containing 21 players rather than the allowed 23, on 5 June 2024.[2]

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK Max Crocombe (1993-08-12)12 August 1993 (aged 30) 6 0 Burton Albion
2 2DF Tim Payne (1994-01-10)10 January 1994 (aged 30) 34 2 Wellington Phoenix
3 2DF Lukas Kelly-Heald (2005-03-18)18 March 2005 (aged 19) 0 0 Wellington Phoenix
4 2DF Tyler Bindon (2005-01-27)27 January 2005 (aged 19) 6 0 Reading
5 2DF Finn Surman (2003-09-03)3 September 2003 (aged 20) 1 0 Wellington Phoenix
6 3MF Cameron Howieson (1994-12-22)22 December 1994 (aged 29) 16 0 Auckland City
7 4FW Kosta Barbarouses (1990-02-19)19 February 1990 (aged 34) 52 4 Wellington Phoenix
8 3MF Alex Rufer (1996-06-12)12 June 1996 (aged 28) 9 0 Wellington Phoenix
9 4FW Ben Waine (2001-06-11)11 June 2001 (aged 23) 13 1 Plymouth Argyle
11 3MF Elijah Just (2000-05-01)1 May 2000 (aged 24) 22 1 Horsens
12 1GK Alex Paulsen (2002-07-04)4 July 2002 (aged 21) 0 0 Wellington Phoenix
13 2DF Liberato Cacace (captain) (2000-09-27)27 September 2000 (aged 23) 22 1 Empoli
14 3MF Ben Old (2002-08-13)13 August 2002 (aged 21) 4 0 Wellington Phoenix
15 2DF Tommy Smith (1990-03-31)31 March 1990 (aged 34) 52 2 Macarthur FC
16 2DF Sam Sutton (2001-12-10)10 December 2001 (aged 22) 0 0 Wellington Phoenix
17 4FW Alex Greive (1999-05-13)13 May 1999 (aged 25) 11 2 Dundee United
18 4FW Oskar van Hattum (2002-04-14)14 April 2002 (aged 22) 0 0 Wellington Phoenix
19 4FW Jesse Randall (2002-08-19)19 August 2002 (aged 21) 0 0 Wellington Olympic
20 3MF Fin Conchie (2003-08-10)10 August 2003 (aged 20) 0 0 Wellington Phoenix
21 4FW Max Mata (2000-07-10)10 July 2000 (aged 23) 10 0 Shrewsbury Town
22 1GK Oliver Sail (1996-01-13)13 January 1996 (aged 28) 9 0 Perth Glory

Solomon Islands

[edit]

Solomon Islands announced their final squad on 29 May 2024.[3]

Head coach: Jacob Moli

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK Philip Mango (1995-08-28)28 August 1995 (aged 28) 37 0 Central Coast
2 2DF Allen Peter (1995-09-11)11 September 1995 (aged 28) 16 0 Solomon Warriors

20 1GK Harold Nauania (1997-10-10)10 October 1997 (aged 26) 1 0 Waneagu United
21 2DF David Supa (2000-12-21)21 December 2000 (aged 23) 11 0 Central Coast
22 2DF Prince Tahanipue (1995-01-13)13 January 1995 (aged 29) 7 0 Central Coast

Vanuatu

[edit]

Vanuatu announced their final squad on 4 June 2024.[4] On 15 June, Dick Sablan was replaced by Joshua Willie.

Head coach: Juliano Schmeling

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK James Iamar (1997-01-25)25 January 1997 (aged 27) 0 0 Tafea
2 2DF Lency Philip (1997-06-08)8 June 1997 (aged 27) 10 0 Classic
3 2DF Timothy Boulet (1998-11-29)29 November 1998 (aged 25) 11 0 Auckland City

20 3MF Alick Worworbu (1997-08-27)27 August 1997 (aged 26) 0 0 Yatel
21 4FW Joe Moses (2002-05-22)22 May 2002 (aged 22) 5 2 ABM Galaxy
22 3MF Barry Mansale (1995-11-01)1 November 1995 (aged 28) 11 1 Yatel
23 1GK Joshua Willie (2000-06-13)13 June 2000 (aged 24) 2 0 ABM Galaxy

Group B

[edit]

Fiji

[edit]

Fiji announced their 25-men preiliminary squad on 24 April 2024.[5] The final squad was announced on 5 June.[6]

Head coach: Rob Sherman

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK Aydin Mustahib (2004-05-28)28 May 2004 (aged 20) 1 0 Manurewa
2 2DF Scott Wara (1999-09-22)22 September 1999 (aged 24) 3 0 Stalybridge Celtic

[edit]

Samoa announced their final squad on 10 June 2024.[8]

Head coach: Ryan Stewart

Tahiti

[edit]

Tahiti announced their final squad, containing 21 players rather than the allowed 23, on 6 June 2024.[9]

Head coach: Samuel Garcia

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club

Statistics

[edit]

By age

[edit]

Players

[edit]
  • Oldest: Andrew Setefano (36 years, 310 days)
  • Youngest: Raymond Diho (17 years, 356 days)

Goalkeepers

[edit]
  • Oldest: Ronald Warisan (34 years, 269 days)
  • Youngest: Kirk Auvele (18 years, 5 days)

Captains

[edit]
  • Oldest: Andrew Setefano (36 years, 310 days)
  • Youngest: Liberato Cacace (23 years, 262 days)

Coaches

[edit]
  • Oldest: Rob Sherman ( Fiji) (63 years, 299 days)
  • Youngest: Ryan Stewart ( Samoa) (39 years, 32 days)

By club nationality

[edit]

Nations in bold are participant nations in the tournament

Players Clubs
28  New Zealand
22  Solomon Islands
19  Tahiti
18  Fiji
17  Vanuatu
16  Papua New Guinea
15  Australia
5  England,  Samoa
2  United States
1  Argentina,  Bosnia and Herzegovina,  Denmark,  France,  Germany,  India,  Italy,  Portugal,  Qatar,  Scotland

References

[edit]
  1. ^ "OFC Men's Nations Cup 2024 Regulations" (PDF). Oceania Football Confederation. Retrieved 3 June 2024.
  2. ^ "Four new names in All Whites squad selected for OFC Nations Cup". New Zealand Football. 5 June 2024.
  3. ^ "Final 23-man squad named for OFC Nations Cup 2024". Facebook. Solomon Islands Football Federation. 29 May 2024.
  4. ^ "Vanuatu 23 Senior Mens Roster for OFC Nations Cup 2024". Twitter. Vanuatu Football Federation. 4 June 2024.
  5. ^ "The DIGICEL Fiji Men's National Extended Squad has been called up for the National Camp next month in preparation for the OFC Nations Cup 2024". Twitter. Fiji Football Association. 24 April 2024.
  6. ^ "The 23-member Digicel Fiji Bula Boys squad for the OFC Nations Cup 2024 which will be hosted at the HFC Bank Stadium in Suva from 16-30 June". Facebook. Fiji Football Association. 5 June 2024.
  7. ^ "Men's Nation Cup squad 2024". Facebook. Papua New Guinea Football Association. 3 June 2024.
  8. ^ "SAMOA LOOK TO MAKE NATIONS CUP IMPACT FOLLOWING QUALIFICATION SUCCESS". Oceaniafootball.com. 10 June 2024.
  9. ^ "Coupe des Nations 2024 de l'OFC : Découvrez la liste officielle des joueurs sélectionnés !". Tahitian Football Federation. 6 June 2024.
